text:
I'll search for this paper to verify the cited metadata.
Based on the web search results, I can now provide a clear assessment:

The search results consistently show that 
the paper was published at the International Conference on Machine Learning with authors Hafner, Danijar and Lillicrap, Timothy and Fischer, Ian and Villegas, Ruben and Ha, David and Lee, Honglak and Davidson, James
. The cited reference lists only 4 authors (Danijar Hafner, Timothy P. Lillicrap, Mohammad Norouzi, Jimmy Ba), but the actual published paper has 7 authors, with the complete author list being: Danijar Hafner, Timothy Lillicrap, Ian Fischer, Ruben Villegas, David Ha, Honglak Lee, and James Davidson. The venue is also incorrect in the citation—it should be the International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML), not the International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR).

The cited reference appears to be a "grafted" hallucination: the title is real and correct, but the author list is substantially different from the actual published paper. Mohammad Norouzi and Jimmy Ba are not among the actual authors, and several real authors are completely missing.

EXPLANATION: The paper exists with this exact title, but the cited authors are incorrect. The actual authors are Hafner, Lillicrap, Fischer, Villegas, Ha, Lee, and Davidson. Mohammad Norouzi and Jimmy Ba are not listed as authors. The venue is also wrong (ICML, not ICLR).

LINK: https://proceedings.mlr.press/v97/hafner19a/hafner19a.pdf

FOUND_TITLE: Learning Latent Dynamics for Planning from Pixels

FOUND_AUTHORS: Danijar Hafner, Timothy Lillicrap, Ian Fischer, Ruben Villegas, David Ha, Honglak Lee, James Davidson

FOUND_YEAR: 2019

VERDICT: LIKELY
